title: USEC_RTINFO
text: USEC_RTINFO Provide runtime information about the negotiated options of the current or previous connection. Use SMTP security drivers Expected Input Properties , Parameters OutBuf , OutBufLen DrvArea Expected Output OutDataLen , ErrMsg , ErrMsgLen DrvArea Return Status USEC_SUCCESS Description The USEC_RTINFO function should be implemented in security drivers that negotiate options with the SMTP mail server. It must provide the result of those negotiations in OutBuf . The USEC_RTINFO function is called when the GetSecurity operation of the UPOPMAIL component is called. The driver specifies whether it supports this function code in the USEC_DRVINFO call. The function should return the negotiated options from the currently established connection or the last one that was used. If no connection has ever been established, it can return zero in OutDataLen . The runtime information must be supplied in the form of a Uniface list of name-value pairs, separated by a byte whose value is 27 ( "name=value [27] name=value" ). You can use the PutItem utility function to assemble this list, writing it to OutBuf . The length of the list should not exceed OutBufLen . The actual length must be provided in OutDataLen . This function code must return success. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
